top_signals.title: Formentera Partners on Oil Markets, Iran War Impact
top_signals.summary: Bryan Sheffield, Managing Partner at Formentera Partners, discusses the impact from the Iran war and outlook for the energy market. He speaks with Paul Allen from the sidelines of the Australian Energy Producers Conference in Adelaide.  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Higher Oil Prices Have Cost U.S. Consumers $45 Billion Since Iran War Began
top_signals.summary: As U.S. drivers face the highest Memorial Day gasoline prices in four years, American consumers are paying billions of dollars more on gas and diesel than they did a year ago.   The global oil supply shock, created by the Iran war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, is affecting Americans disproportionately. The lower-income households are being hit the most as their purchasing power is being eroded by $45 billion in extra costs on fuel compared to a year ago. At the other end of the spectrum, affluent Americans see their financial assets…

top_signals.title: Premier League clubs’ success could earn HMRC £40m windfall
top_signals.summary: Premier League clubs’ unprecedented success in Europe this season could earn HMRC a £40m windfall, according to tax experts. Bonuses paid out to players for winning domestic and continental trophies could hit £70m, generating a lucrative pot for the taxman, if Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace clean up in the next two weeks.  The [...]
